predictions.description: passCount at snapshot time is 7 = 3 explicit (add-to-pass-count default 1, then +2) + 4 engine auto-increments (the engine bumps passCount for EVERY passing step, and exactly steps 1-4 have completed when the snapshot runs; the snapshot step itself is not yet counted). This pins the coupling between the action and the engine's per-step pass counting.
failCount is exactly the explicit +1 — no step has failed, so the engine's per-step fail counting contributes nothing.
transactionCount is exactly the explicit +5 — the engine never auto-increments transactions.

reset-to-default restores the declared defaultValue after a set-variable changed it; snapChanged holds the pre-reset value 'changed-value', proving the set really happened before the reset (the final DEFAULT-VALUE is not just 'nothing ever ran').
reset-to-default on a list restores the full default array [x, y] after add-to-list had appended z — reset covers structured values, not just text.
